description: Add a product favorites feature: authenticated users can click a heart icon on the single-product page (and product cards) in the store micro-frontend to add/remove a product from their favorites list. Their favorites list is persisted via the Catalog service (MongoDB) and is viewable on a dedicated /favorites route in the account micro-frontend. Done when: heart icon toggles correctly on store product pages, favorites are persisted to the backend and retrieved by user name, and the account /favorites page lists the user's saved products.

userStories.title: Favorites data model and API endpoints in Catalog service
userStories.description:
As a backend developer, I need to persist user favorites in MongoDB and expose CRUD endpoints so the frontend can add, remove, and list favorites per user.

Implementation:
- Add `FavoriteProduct` entity to `Services/Catalog/Catalog.Core/Entities/FavoriteProduct.cs` with fields: `Id` (string), `UserName` (string), `ProductId` (string), `ProductName` (string), `ProductImageUrl` (string nullable), `CreatedAt` (DateTime).
- Add `IFavoriteRepository` to `Services/Catalog/Catalog.Core/Repositories/IFavoriteRepository.cs` (methods: `AddFavoriteAsync`, `RemoveFavoriteAsync`, `GetFavoritesByUserNameAsync`, `IsFavoriteAsync`).
- Add `FavoriteRepository` to `Services/Catalog/Catalog.Infrastructure/Repositories/FavoriteRepository.cs` using MongoDB.Driver against collection `Favorites` in the existing `CatalogDb`.
- Register `FavoriteRepository` in `Services/Catalog/Catalog.API/Program.cs` (scoped, same pattern as `ProductRepository`).
- Add CQRS in `Catalog.Application`:
  - `Commands/AddFavoriteCommand.cs` (properties: `UserName`, `ProductId`, `ProductName`, `ProductImageUrl`) → returns `FavoriteProductResponse`
  - `Commands/RemoveFavoriteCommand.cs` (property: `FavoriteId`) → returns `bool`
  - `Queries/GetFavoritesByUserNameQuery.cs` (property: `UserName`) → returns `IEnumerable<FavoriteProductResponse>`
  - `Handlers/AddFavoriteHandler.cs`, `RemoveFavoriteHandler.cs`, `GetFavoritesByUserNameHandler.cs`
  - `Responses/FavoriteProductResponse.cs`
- Expose in `CatalogController`:
  - `POST /api/v1/Catalog/favorites` body: `AddFavoriteCommand` → returns 200 with `FavoriteProductResponse`
  - `DELETE /api/v1/Catalog/favorites/{favoriteId}` → returns 200
  - `GET /api/v1/Catalog/favorites/{userName}` → returns 200 with list of `FavoriteProductResponse`
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/v1/Catalog/favorites with valid body returns 200 with FavoriteProductResponse including an Id
GET /api/v1/Catalog/favorites/{userName} returns a list of favorites for that user
DELETE /api/v1/Catalog/favorites/{favoriteId} removes the entry and returns 200
Favorites are persisted in MongoDB Favorites collection
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the existing handler pattern in Catalog.Application/Handlers. MongoDB.Driver is already referenced — no new NuGet packages needed. The Favorites collection is created automatically by MongoDB on first insert.

userStories.title: Wire favorites routes through Ocelot API gateway
userStories.description:
As a gateway operator, I need to add three new Ocelot routes so frontend apps can reach the Catalog favorites endpoints through the gateway at :8010.

Files to update:
- `ApiGateways/Ocelot.ApiGateway/ocelot.Development.json`
- `ApiGateways/Ocelot.ApiGateway/ocelot.k8s.json`

Add three route entries matching the same pattern as existing Catalog routes:
- `GET /catalog/favorites/{userName}` → upstream `http://catalog.api/api/v1/Catalog/favorites/{userName}`
- `POST /catalog/favorites` → upstream `http://catalog.api/api/v1/Catalog/favorites`
- `DELETE /catalog/favorites/{favoriteId}` → upstream `http://catalog.api/api/v1/Catalog/favorites/{favoriteId}`
acceptanceCriteria: GET /catalog/favorites/{userName} proxied correctly in ocelot.Development.json
POST /catalog/favorites proxied correctly in ocelot.Development.json
DELETE /catalog/favorites/{favoriteId} proxied correctly in ocelot.Development.json
Corresponding routes added to ocelot.k8s.json
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Inspect existing Catalog GET/POST/DELETE routes in ocelot.Development.json for the exact UpstreamPathTemplate, DownstreamScheme, and DownstreamHostAndPorts to replicate. k8s variant uses service DNS names.

userStories.title: Favorite toggle icon on product cards in store micro-frontend
userStories.description:
As a shopper, I want to click a heart icon on a product card to add/remove that product from my favorites so I can track products I like.

Implementation in `micro-frontends/store/src/`:
- Add `src/services/favorites/` with:
  - `apis.ts` — Axios calls using the same httpClient pattern as `src/services/products/apis.ts`: `addFavorite(body)` POST to `/catalog/favorites`, `removeFavorite(favoriteId)` DELETE to `/catalog/favorites/{favoriteId}`, `getFavoritesByUserName(userName)` GET `/catalog/favorites/{userName}`
  - `keys.ts` — TanStack Query keys
  - `hooks.ts` — `useAddFavorite()`, `useRemoveFavorite()`, `useFavoritesByUserName(userName)` using TanStack Query
  - `schemas.ts` — Zod schema for `FavoriteProductResponse` ({id, userName, productId, productName, productImageUrl, createdAt})
  - `index.ts` — barrel export
- Add `src/components/FavoriteButton.tsx` — uses `HeartOutlined`/`HeartFilled` from `@ant-design/icons`; accepts `productId`, `productName`, `productImageUrl?` props; reads current favorites from `useFavoritesByUserName(user?.name)` via `useAuth()`; hidden/disabled when `!isAuthenticated`.
- Integrate `FavoriteButton` into `src/pages/ProductList.tsx` on each product card.
acceptanceCriteria: A heart icon is visible on each product card in ProductList
Heart icon is hidden/disabled when user is not authenticated
Clicking the heart icon when authenticated calls POST /catalog/favorites and turns the icon filled
Clicking again calls DELETE /catalog/favorites/{favoriteId} and returns the icon to outline
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: screenshot showing filled heart on a favorited product and empty heart on a non-favorited product
userStories.notes: Use NX_API_BASE_URL as the base for favorites API calls — same pattern as src/services/products/apis.ts. useAuth() from @ecommerce-platform/auth-provider provides user.name (userName) and isAuthenticated. Check the exact user shape from the existing auth hooks.

userStories.title: Favorites list page in account micro-frontend
userStories.description:
As a signed-in user, I want to see a list of my favorited products on my account page so I can review and remove them.

Implementation in `micro-frontends/account/src/`:
- Add `src/services/favorites/` with:
  - `apis.ts` — `getFavoritesByUserName(userName)` and `removeFavorite(favoriteId)` via `src/http/httpClient.ts`
  - `keys.ts` — query keys
  - `hooks.ts` — `useFavoritesByUserName(userName)`, `useRemoveFavorite()`
  - `schemas.ts` — Zod schema for `FavoriteProductResponse`
  - `index.ts` — barrel export
- Add route file `src/routes/favorites.tsx` (file-based routing — same pattern as `orders.tsx`)
- Add page component `src/pages/Favorites.tsx` — lists favorites with product name, image thumbnail, and a remove button using Ant Design `List` or `Card.Grid`. Must handle loading/empty/error states.
- Run `npm run routes:generate` from `micro-frontends/` root to regenerate `src/routeTree.gen.ts` after creating the route file.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigating to /account/favorites renders the Favorites page
The page lists favorited products with name and image
Clicking remove calls DELETE /catalog/favorites/{favoriteId} and refreshes the list
Empty state shown when no favorites exist
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser: screenshot of the Favorites page with at least one product listed
userStories.notes: IMPORTANT: run `npm run routes:generate` from micro-frontends/ after creating src/routes/favorites.tsx — routeTree.gen.ts is generated and must not be edited manually. httpClient in src/http/httpClient.ts already injects the auth token. useAuth() provides user.name for the userName parameter.
